data.description:
RNS Number : 8193L JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities 06 June 2025  

JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC (the 'Company')

Legal Entity Identifier: 549300II3MHI98ZLVH37

THE COMPANY ANNOUNCES THE UNAUDITED NET ASSET VALUE (NAV) As at:  05 June 2025

THE NET ASSET VALUE PER SHARE IN PENCE, INCLUDING INCOME WITH DEBT AT FAIR VALUE:

56.87

The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.

data.title: Hydrogen Utopia International Signs Deal to Access InEnTec's Plasma Technology in MENA
data.description:
Hydrogen Utopia International (, HUI.AQ) said Friday it signed a heads of terms agreement with InEnTec to access 10 exclusive licenses for its TRL9 waste-to-hydrogen technology across the Middle East and North Africa region.

The waste plastic-to-hydrogen production company added that each facility is projected to cost between $50 million and $100 million, with HUI retaining a minority stake in the special purpose vehicles linked to the projects and receiving an ongoing management fee for strategic oversight and operational support.

Hydrogen Utopia International expects the plasma-enhanced melter technology to allow it to partner with steel and cement companies in the region, enabling the construction of facilities.

data.title: Mitsubishi Paper Mills Restructures German Subsidiary, Including Voluntary Retirement
data.description:
Mitsubishi Paper Mills' German consolidated subsidiary, Mitsubishi Hi-tec Paper Europe, is implementing a business restructuring plan, including a voluntary retirement program, according to its Tokyo bourse filing on Thursday.

This decision stems from ongoing economic stagnation in Europe and intense price competition in the thermal paper market, despite continuous efforts to improve profitability after the Flensburg mill's transfer in 2022.

The voluntary retirement program, open to approximately 140 MPE employees, has an application period from June 4-11, with retirement effective June 30.

Preferential treatment, including severance payments, will be offered, and applicants can utilize Germany's reemployment support system starting July 1.

data.title: China's Downward Price Pressures Likely Worsened in May, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: China's consumer and factory-gate prices likely fell further last month, according to a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal, indicating weaker domestic demand amid looming trade barriers. The consumer-price index is expected to have slipped 0.2% on year in May, compared with the previous month's 0.1% decline, the poll showed. Producer prices probably slid 3.2% on year, worsening from April's 2.7% drop. China's National Bureau of Statistics is slated to release the data on Monday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Price Falls. Why the Circle IPO Success Isn't Boosting Cryptos. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Brian Swint

Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were trading lower Friday as they broadly tracked sentiment on the stock market.

Cryptos often trade like high-risk technology stocks, though sometimes they are also seen as a haven asset like gold.

For the past 24 hours, they've traded in line with the S&P 500--falling most of Thursday after President Donald Trump's call with Chinese leader Xi Jinping that didn't yield a tariffs breakthrough. Trump's public spat with billionaire Tesla CEO Elon Musk may have also weighed on the market. But by early Friday, they were starting to make a recovery.

Crypto got a boost as an asset class on Thursday as Circle Internet Group, which issues a coin tied to the value of the dollar, soared 168% on its first day of trading. However, that didn't seem to translate to cryptocurrencies prices Thursday, which instead followed the stock market.

The May report on nonfarm payrolls could have a big impact on cryptos and stocks when it is published later this morning.

Bitcoin was down 1.2% over the past 24 hours at $103,707, though up from the lows hit late Thursday. Ethereum, the second-biggest cryptocurrency, was down 5.5%, while XRP was 2.5% lower. Solana traded 2.7% down, and Cardano slipped 5.7%.

Write to Brian Swint at brian.swint@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
